segments.text: Around this time there was another large crowd that had nothing to eat. Jesus called the disciples together and told them,
I feel for the crowd because they have already stayed with me for three days and they have nothing to eat.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If I send them home without food, they'll faint on the way. Some have come from a long way away.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Where could anybody find enough bread to feed them here in this wilderness?
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: answered his disciples.
How many loaves do you have?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: he asked.
Seven,
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: they replied.
He told the crowd to sit down on the ground. Then he took the seven loaves of bread, and gave thanks. He broke the bread and handed the pieces to his disciples to give to the crowd.
They had a few fish as well, so having blessed them, he said, {
Take these and share them too.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They ate until they were full, and then collected up seven baskets of leftovers.
There were four thousand people there. After sending them on their way,
Jesus climbed into a boat with his disciples and went to the region of Dalmanutha.
The Pharisees arrived and began to argue with him, wanting him to give them a miraculous sign from heaven, trying to get him to prove himself.
Jesus sighed deeply, and asked, {
Why do you people[^36] want a sign? I tell you the truth: I will not give you a sign.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So he left them behind, climbed into the boat, and went back across the lake.
But the disciples had forgotten to bring bread with them. All they had in the boat was one loaf.
Watch out—beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: he warned them.
He's saying that because we don't have any bread,
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: they concluded.
Jesus knew what they were saying, and said, {
Why are you talking about not having any bread? Are you still not thinking and not understanding? Have you closed your minds?[^37]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You've got eyes to see, haven't you? And ears to hear?[^38]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Don't you remember when I shared five loaves among five thousand? How many basketfuls of leftovers did you pick up?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Twelve,
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: they replied.
And the seven loaves divided among four thousand. How many basketfuls of leftovers did you pick up?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Seven,
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: they answered.
Do you still not understand?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: he asked them.
They went to Bethsaida where some people brought a blind man to Jesus. They begged Jesus to touch him and heal him.
Jesus took the blind man by the hand and took him outside the village. Jesus spat on the man's eyes and touched him with his hands. Then Jesus asked him, {
Can you see anything?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The man looked around, and said, {
I can see people, but they look like trees walking.
segments.speaker: The man looked around, and

segments.text: So Jesus touched the man's eyes again. He could see properly—he was cured and his sight was clear.
Jesus sent the man home, and told him, {
Don't go back through the village.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus and his disciples left to go to the villages of Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, {
Who do people say I am?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Some say John the Baptist, some say Elijah, others say one of the prophets,
segments.speaker: The disciples

segments.text: they replied.
But who do you say I am?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: he asked them.
You are the Messiah!
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: Peter answered.
Jesus warned them not to tell anybody about him.

Then Jesus began to explain to them that the Son of man would suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and religious teachers. He would be killed, but three days later would rise again.
Jesus explained this to them very clearly. But Peter took Jesus aside and started to reprimand him for what he said.
Jesus turned around and looking at his disciples, reprimanded Peter. {
Get away from me, Satan,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: he said.
You're not thinking as God thinks, but as human beings do.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus called the crowd and his disciples over to him, and told them, {
If you want to follow me, you must give up on yourselves, pick up your cross and follow me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you want to save your life you will lose it, but if you lose your life because of me and the good news you will save it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What use is it for you to gain everything in the whole world, and lose your life?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What would you give in exchange for your life?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you're ashamed to acknowledge[^40] me and what I say among this unfaithful and sinful people, [^41] then the Son of man will be ashamed to acknowledge you when he comes with his Father's glory with the holy angels.
segments.speaker: Jesus
